generated_oml:
instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "A 1/10-scale teleoperated autonomous vehicle retrofitted with advanced sensor and control technology, featuring lower and upper chassis layers and autonomy elements. The system operates in the Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) domain with resource-constrained electronic control units (ECUs), necessitating computation offloading for safety-critical operations and ADAS development."
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity, 3DVisualization, ControlPanel
]

instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity handling data pre-processing, actuation, device communication, and Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking"
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital Twin Entity responsible for simulation and data processing"
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User Entity deployed on separate hardware containing User Interface with 3D Visualization and Control Panel subcomponents"
]

instance 3DVisualization : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "3D Visualization subcomponent for real-time display of vehicle state and object tracks"
]

instance ControlPanel : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Control Panel subcomponent for operator input and command issuance"
]

instance LidarSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor capturing raw point cloud data for obstacle detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LidarPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing vehicle speed, position, and orientation data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringInputSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing steering angle and input commands"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringInputDataTransmitted
]

instance ServoInputSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing servo motor control signals"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoInputDataTransmitted
]

instance LidarPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LidarSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance SteeringInputDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringInputSensor
]

instance ServoInputDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoInputSensor
]

instance LidarPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted over Wi-Fi UDP"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LidarPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2CommunicationEnabler
]

instance SteeringInputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering angle and input commands data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringInputDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2CommunicationEnabler
]

instance ServoInputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Servo motor control signals data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ServoInputDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2CommunicationEnabler
]

instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component for processing raw LiDAR point clouds into clustered and tracked object data"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component for data serialization, message type alignment, and unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance ROS2CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 middleware for internal communication between software components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data, projected driving path, detected object tracks via UI with reporting capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateVisualization, ProjectedDrivingPathVisualization, DetectedObjectTracks
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "ACC system lead vehicle detection, speed calculation based on distance, and actuation commands offloaded to cloud resources"
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetection, SpeedCalculation, ActuationCommandOffload
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote enable/disable ACC, desired speed setting, emergency braking commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteEnableDisableACC, SetDesiredSpeed, EmergencyBrakingCommand
]

instance RealTimeVehicleStateVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle speed, position, and orientation data"
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of the vehicle's projected driving path based on ACC system"
]

instance DetectedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of detected and tracked objects in the vehicle's environment"
]

instance LeadVehicleDetection : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of lead vehicle for ACC system"
]

instance SpeedCalculation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Calculation of speed based on distance to lead vehicle"
]

instance ActuationCommandOffload : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offloading of actuation commands to cloud resources for ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteEnableDisableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote command to enable or disable Adaptive Cruise Control system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote command to set desired speed for ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote emergency braking command sent to vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) are processed by the Controlling FE, serialized via the Data Translation component, and transmitted over Wi-Fi UDP to the Actuation FE for motor and steering servo actuation. Command types beyond ACC, message formats, validation/authentication mechanisms, feedback systems, and execution confirmation details are not specified."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system requires 100 ms clock synchronization accuracy for computation offloading between devices, adhering to safety-critical vehicular standards but constrained by hardware limitations."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The architecture follows ISO 23247 RA, organizing software components into three entities mapped to ROS2 packages. Each entity is deployed on dedicated hardware: Physical Twin Entity (handling general automotive functions like Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking), Digital Twin Entity (data management and control), and User Entity (3D Visualization and Control Panel). This deviates from ISO 23247 by extending the Physical Twin Entity beyond the standard's OME to encompass broader system resources."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Internal software components communicate via ROS2, while inter-entity communication uses Wi-Fi with UDP transport layer. Network topology, bandwidth, latency requirements, reliability mechanisms, and security protocols are not detailed in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Compliance with ISO 23247:2021 Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing standard, which includes four parts: Overview and general principles (Part 1), Reference architecture (Part 2), Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements (Part 3), and Information exchange (Part 4). Part 2 defines an entity-based reference architecture comprising Observable Manufacturing Elements (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. Functional Entities (FEs) implement specific tasks, with OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) cited as a possible implementation for interoperability."
]
